author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm CXX

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Psalm which we have just heard chanted, and have responded to with our voices, is short, and very profitable.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Ye will not long toil in hearing, nor will ye toil fruitlessly in working.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For it is, according to the title prefixed to it, “A song of degrees.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Degrees are either of ascent or of descent.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But degrees, as they are used in this Psalm, are of ascending…There are therefore both those who ascend and those who descend on that ladder.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who are they that ascend?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They who progress towards the understanding of things spiritual.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who are they that descend?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They who, although, as far as men may, they enjoy the comprehension of things spiritual: nevertheless, descend unto the infants, to say to them such things as they can receive, so that, after being nourished with milk, they may become fitted and strong enough to take spiritual meat…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When therefore a man hath commenced thus to order his ascent; to speak more plainly, when a Christian hath begun to think of spiritual amendment, he beginneth to suffer the tongues of adversaries.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whoever hath not yet suffered from them, hath not yet made progress; whoever suffereth them not, doth not even endeavour to improve.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Doth he wish to know what we mean?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let him at the same time experience what is reported of us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let him begin to improve, let him begin to wish to ascend, to wish to despise earthly, fragile, temporal objects, to hold worldly happiness for nothing, to think of God alone, not to rejoice in gain, not to pine at losses, to wish even to sell all his substance, and distribute it among the poor, and to follow Christ; let us see how he suffereth the tongues of detractors and of constant opponents, and—a still greater peril—of pretended counsellors, who lead him astray from salvation…He then, who will ascend, first of all prayeth God against these very tongues: for he saith, “When I was in trouble, I called on the Lord; and He heard me” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why did He hear him?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That He might now place him at the steps of ascent.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Deliver my soul, O Lord, from unrighteous lips, and from a deceitful tongue” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is a deceitful tongue?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A treacherous tongue, one that hath the semblance of counsel, and the bane of real mischief.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Such are those who say, And wilt thou do this, that nobody doth?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wilt thou be the only Christian?…Some deter by dissuasion, others discourage yet more by their praise.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For since such is the life that hath for some time been diffused over the world, so great is the authority of Christ, that not even a pagan ventureth to blame Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He who cannot be censured is read.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They cannot contradict Christ, they cannot contradict the Gospel, Christ cannot be censured; the deceitful tongue turneth itself to praise as an hindrance.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If thou praisest, exhort.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why dost thou discourage with thy praise?…Thou turnest thyself to another mode of dissuasion, that by false praise thou mayest turn me away from true praise; nay, that by praising Christ thou mayest keep me away from Christ, saying, What is this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold these men have done this: thou, perhaps, wilt not be able: thou beginnest to ascend, thou fallest.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It seemeth to warn thee: it is the serpent, it is the deceitful tongue, it hath poison.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Pray against it, if thou wishest to ascend.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And thy Lord saith unto thee, “What shall be given thee, or what shall be set before thee, against the deceitful tongue?” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What shall be given thee, that is, as a weapon to oppose to the deceitful tongue, to guard thyself against the deceitful tongue?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Or what shall be set before thee?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He asketh to try thee: for He will answer His own question.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He answers following up his own inquiry, “even sharp arrows of the Mighty One, with coals that desolate, or that lay waste” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They that desolate, or that lay waste (for it is variously written in different copies), are the same, because by laying waste, as ye may observe, they easily lead unto desolation.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What are these coals?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: First, beloved brethren, understand what are arrows.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The “sharp arrows of the Mighty One,” are the words of God…What then are the “coals that lay waste?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is not enough to plead with words against a deceitful tongue and unrighteous lips: it is not enough to plead with words; we must plead with examples also…The word coals, then, is used to express the examples of many sinners converted to the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou hearest men wonder, and say, I knew that man, how addicted he was to drinking, what a villain, what a lover of the circus, or of the amphitheatre, what a cheat: now how he serveth God, how innocent he hath become!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wonder not; he is a live coal.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou rejoicest that he is alive, whom thou wast mourning as dead.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But when thou praisest the living, if thou knowest how to praise, apply him to the dead, that he may be inflamed; whosoever is still slow to follow God, apply to him the coal which was extinguished, and have the arrow of God’s word, and the coal that layeth waste, that thou mayest meet the deceitful tongue and the lying lips.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Alas, that my sojourning is become far off!” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It hath departed far from Thee: my pilgrimage hath become a far one.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I have not yet reached that country, where I shall live with no wicked person; I have not yet reached that company of Angels, where I shall not fear offences.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But why am I not as yet there?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because sojourning is pilgrimage.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He is called a sojourner who dwells in a foreign land, not in his own country.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And when is it far off?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Sometimes, my brethren, when a man goeth abroad, he liveth among better persons, than he would perhaps live with in his own country: but it is not thus, when we go afar from that heavenly Jerusalem.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For a man changeth his country, and this foreign sojourn is sometimes good for him; in travelling he findeth faithful friends, whom he could not find in his own country.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He had enemies, so that he was driven from his country; and when he travelled, he found what he had not in his country.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Such is not that country Jerusalem, where all are good: whoever travelleth away from thence, is among the evil; nor can he depart from the wicked, save when he shall return to the company of Angels, so as to be where he was before he travelled.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There all are righteous and holy, who enjoy the word of God without reading, without letters: for what is written to us through pages, they perceive there through the Face of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What a country!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A great country indeed, and wretched are the wanderers from that country.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But what he saith, “My pilgrimage hath been made distant,” are the words of those, that is, of the Church herself, who toileth on this earth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is her voice, which crieth out from the ends of the earth in another Psalm, saying, “From the ends of the earth have I cried unto Thee.…Where then doth he groan, and among whom doth he dwell?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I have had my habitation among the tents of Kedar.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Since this is a Hebrew word, beyond doubt ye have not understood it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What meaneth, “I have had my habitation among the tents of Kedar”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Kedar,” as far as we remember of the interpretation of Hebrew words, signifieth darkness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Kedar” rendered into Latin is called tenebræ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now ye know that Abraham had two sons, whom indeed the Apostle mentioneth, and declareth them to have been types of the two covenants…Ishmael therefore was in darkness, Isaac in light.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whoever here also seek earthly felicity in the Church, from God, shall belong to Ishmael.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These are the very persons who gainsay the spiritual ones who are progressing, and detract from them, and have deceitful tongues and unrighteous lips.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Against these the Psalmist, when ascending, prayed, and hot coals that lay waste, and swift and sharp arrows of the Mighty One, were given him for his defence.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For among these he still liveth, until the whole floor be winnowed: he therefore said, “I have dwelt among the tents of Kedar.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The tents of Ishmael are called those of Kedar.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thus the book of Genesis hath it: thus it hath, that Kedar belongeth unto Ishmael.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Isaac therefore is with Ishmael: that is, they who belong unto Isaac, live among those who belong unto Ishmael.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These wish to rise above, those wish to press them downwards: these wish to fly unto God, those endeavour to pluck their wings…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “My soul hath wandered much” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Lest thou shouldest understand bodily wandering, he hath said that the soul wandered.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The body wandereth in places, the soul wandereth in its affections.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If thou love the earth, thou wanderest from God: if thou lovest God, thou risest unto God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us be exercised in the love of God, and of our neighbour, that we may return unto charity.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If we fall towards the earth, we wither and decay.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But one descended unto this one who had fallen, in order that he might arise.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Speaking of the time of his wandering, he said that he wandered in the tents of Kedar.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because “my soul hath wandered much.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He wandereth there where he ascendeth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He wandereth not in the body, he riseth not in the body.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But wherein doth he ascend?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The ascent,” he saith, “is in the heart.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “With them that hated peace, I was peaceful” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But howsoever ye may hear, most beloved brethren, ye will not be able to prove how truly ye sing, unless ye have begun to do that which ye sing.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How much soever I say this, in whatsoever ways I may expound it, in whatsoever words I may turn it, it entereth not into the heart of him in whom its operation is not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Begin to act, and see what we speak.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Then tears flow forth at each word, then the Psalm is sung, and the heart doeth what is sung in the Psalm…Who are they who hate peace?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They who tear asunder unity.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For had they not hated peace, they would have abode in unity.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But they separated themselves, forsooth on this account, that they might be righteous, that they might not have the ungodly mixed with them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These words are either ours or theirs: decide whose.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Catholic Church saith, Unity must not be lost, the Church of God must not be cut off.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: God will judge afterwards of the wicked and the good…This we also say: Love ye peace, love ye Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For if they love peace, they love Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When therefore we say, Love ye peace, we say this, Love ye Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the Apostle saith of Christ, “He is our peace, who hath made both one.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If Christ is therefore peace, because He hath made both one: why have ye made two of one?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How then are ye peace-makers, if, when Christ maketh one of two, ye make two of one?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But since we say these things, we are peace-makers with them that hate peace; and yet they who hate peace, when we spake to them, made war on us for nought.
